text: Ever wonder why we only hear what we want? Meet myside bias. It's when we favor our side's arguments, ignoring anything that contradicts them. But wait! There's more. Confirmation bias is the sneaky cousin. It drives us to seek out evidence that confirms our beliefs. Both are traps. Research suggests these biases can distort our reality, affecting decisions from politics to personal relationships. Awareness is key. Recognizing these traps helps us think clearer. Curious about your own biases? Start questioning your beliefs today! Like and follow for more insights.
